title: Kotlin Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Kotlin source files contain code written in Kotlin, a statically typed JVM language developed by JetBrains that is fully interoperable with Java. Kotlin is the preferred language for Android development and is also used for server-side JVM applications and Kotlin Multiplatform targets.

## When to use
Use [Kotlin Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:kotlin;textable;code) for `.kt` source files and `.kts` Kotlin script files. Kotlin Script (`.kts`) is used for Gradle build files and standalone scripts that do not require a compilation step; implementations may need to distinguish these from regular source.

## Notes
Kotlin compiles to JVM bytecode, JavaScript, or native binaries depending on the target. The language is null-safe by default, with nullable types expressed explicitly. Kotlin Multiplatform allows sharing code across JVM, iOS, and JS targets from a single source set, which can complicate source analysis that assumes a single compilation target.
